title: Easily take an entire YouTube playlist and turn it into high quality transcripts
summary: This repo understands that bulk YouTube transcription needs to be local-first (Whisper), GPU-accelerated (CUDA/faster-whisper), and playlist-aware.  It demonstrates the complete pipeline: YouTube URL -> audio download -> Whisper transcription -> NLP sentence splitting (SpaCy or regex) -> structured output (text/CSV/JSON with timestamps and confidence scores).
creator_view: This repo understands that bulk YouTube transcription needs to be local-first (Whisper), GPU-accelerated (CUDA/faster-whisper), and playlist-aware. It demonstrates the complete pipeline: YouTube URL -> audio download -> Whisper transcription -> NLP sentence splitting (SpaCy or regex) -> structured output (text/CSV/JSON with timestamps and confidence scores). The transcript reader HTML shows UX awareness beyond raw transcription. It's the practical, local-first complement to AWS Transcribe approaches — exactly the T27 reference for Whisper-based extraction.

candidates.name: faster-whisper local transcription pipeline
candidates.description: Complete local Whisper lifecycle: CUDA detection, large-v3, beam_size=10, vad_filter, segment iteration with tqdm, metadata capture. T27 reference.

candidates.name: Whisper tuned params (beam_size=10, vad_filter)
candidates.description: beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True prevents hallucination-on-silence. Production tuning.

candidates.name: Caption-first + Whisper-fallback architecture
candidates.description: Cross-repo insight: both bedrock and this repo skip captions. pytubefix has yt.captions. Caption-first for ~80% of videos, Whisper fallback for rest.

candidates.name: Async download + sync GPU transcription
candidates.description: asyncio.Semaphore(4) for concurrent downloads, sequential GPU transcription. Network parallelizes, GPU doesn't.

candidates.name: faster-whisper library
candidates.description: CTranslate2 Whisper. 4x faster than OpenAI whisper. GPU+CPU. THE local transcription engine for T27.

candidates.name: CUDA/GPU detection + CPU fallback
candidates.description: numba.cuda.is_available(), Anaconda toolkit paths, float16 vs auto compute. Real-world GPU setup.

candidates.name: SpaCy + compromise.js two-stage NLP
candidates.description: Server-side SpaCy for sentence splitting, client-side compromise.js for paragraph restructuring. Two NLP stages.

candidates.name: API vs local cost tradeoff
candidates.description: $0.006/min API (less accurate) vs free local (more accurate, needs GPU). 1h = $0.36 vs $0.

candidates.name: Monolithic single-file tool anti-pattern
candidates.description: 280 lines, no CLI, no config file. Works for personal use, impossible to integrate. T27 must be modular.

candidates.name: Skip-captions-always anti-pattern
candidates.description: Both bedrock and this repo transcribe every video from scratch. Check captions first (~80% have them).

candidates.name: Hard-coded source config anti-pattern
candidates.description: Module-level variables including API keys. No CLI args, no env vars.

repo.description: Easily take an entire YouTube playlist and turn it into high quality transcripts using Whisper.

QS-01_activity_recency.detail: Last push Feb 2025 (~14 months ago). 24 total commits.

QS-02_engagement.detail: 664 stars, 82 forks. Popular utility for its niche.

QS-03_archived_abandoned.detail: Not archived. Still getting stars (last updated Apr 2026). Functionally complete.

QS-17_fork_to_star.detail: 82/664 = 0.12. Lower fork ratio — people star but don't modify.
